businessSummary.oneLiner: Government and enterprise AI data analytics platform leader with Gotham (defense), Foundry (enterprise), and AIP (AI Platform)
businessSummary.description: Palantir builds AI data analytics platforms for government and enterprise clients. FY2025 revenue hit $4.48B (+56%), with US commercial revenue crossing $500M as civilian market expansion succeeds. The company recently expanded into naval production AI through the ShipOS project. Despite 1,860% stock gains over 3 years, it experienced 10+ drawdowns of 20%+.

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: 10x S&P 500 avg (22x). Still extreme even after price decline

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 EPS of $1.34. Growth premium priced in but remains elevated

keyMetrics.name: P/S Ratio
keyMetrics.interpretation: Investors paying ~$77 per dollar of revenue. Typical SaaS is 10-15x

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Top-tier among SaaS. Achieving high profitability alongside high growth

keyMetrics.name: FCF Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: FCF $2.1B. Software industry's best cash generation

growthDrivers.title: Moder AI Mortgage Partnership: Financial Services Entry
growthDrivers.description: Freedom Mortgage as first pilot for AI mortgage operations platform. Proving commercial expansion beyond defense.

growthDrivers.title: Mizuho: 'Unlike Anything Else in Software,' Outperform $195
growthDrivers.description: Mizuho reiterated after management meeting. Called Palantir incomparable in the software industry.

growthDrivers.title: UBS $200 Raise + Wedbush $230 Maintained
growthDrivers.description: UBS Karl Keirstead raised from $180 to $200 (+32%). Wedbush $230 Buy maintained.

growthDrivers.title: US Navy ShipOS $448M: AI for Submarine Production
growthDrivers.description: Keel joins Palantir on ShipOS project. AI now applied to submarine and naval production lines.

growthDrivers.title: $11.2B Revenue Backlog + $10B Army Contract
growthDrivers.description: $10B Army contract reshaping defense software market. Multi-year revenue visibility secured.

growthDrivers.title: FY2026 Revenue $7.18-7.20B (+60%), Growth Accelerating
growthDrivers.description: From $4.48B in FY2025. Rule of 40 score of 127%.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 20+ years in government and defense AI. Security clearances create entry barriers. ShipOS extends AI to military production.

moats.description: Highest-level clearances with DoD and intelligence agencies. Competitors cannot enter.
AI deployed in submarine production and naval shipyards. Beyond battlefield to military manufacturing.
Sovereign AI OS + NVIDIA hardware ecosystem. Setting government AI infrastructure standards globally.

competitors.name: Microsoft (Azure AI)
competitors.detail: Cloud AI platform. General approach vs Palantir's specialized analytics

competitors.name: Snowflake
competitors.detail: Data lakehouse expanding into analytics platforms

competitors.name: C3.ai
competitors.detail: Enterprise AI. Trailing Palantir in scale and track record

recentNews.headline: Guardian: Palantir Sues Small Swiss Magazine, Press Freedom Controversy
recentNews.significance: Filed right-of-reply lawsuit in Swiss court. Guardian calls it an 'intimidation campaign.' NHS hospital group chair also stepping down over Palantir advisory role. European public contract risk rising.

recentNews.headline: Mizuho Reiterates Outperform $195: 'Unlike Anything Else in Software'
recentNews.significance: Post-management meeting. Strongest qualitative assessment. Consensus at $196.32.

recentNews.headline: Palantir and Moder Partner for AI Mortgage Platform
recentNews.significance: Freedom Mortgage pilot. Proving commercial expansion from defense into financial services.

recentNews.headline: Pentagon Pushing to Remove Anthropic Claude from Maven in 6 Months
recentNews.significance: Military users push back on replacement. Maven contract $1B+. Transition uncertainty.

recentNews.headline: UBS Raises Target to $200, Buy Maintained

analystOpinions.notableComment: UBS $200(+32%), Wedbush $230(+52%), Mizuho $195 reiterated. 16 of 28 Buy. Price drop widened upside to 30%.

risks.title: Extreme Valuation: P/E 222x, P/S 77x
risks.description: 10x S&P 500 average. FCF yield below 1%. Still extremely expensive even after the decline.

risks.title: Swiss Lawsuit + NHS Adviser Exit: European Risk Rising
risks.description: Guardian reports Palantir's Swiss lawsuit as 'intimidation campaign.' NHS hospital group chair stepping down over advisory role. European public contract risk intensifying.

risks.title: Insider Selling Continues
risks.description: CEO Karp sold $6.6M, Stephen Cohen $43.7M. Management actively taking profits at current levels.

risks.title: Pentagon Anthropic Claude Removal Risk
risks.description: Maven $1B+ contract AI engine transition could be costly and time-consuming.

risks.title: Stock Dilution
risks.description: ~5% annual dilution from SBC. Erodes long-term per-share value.

risks.title: Middle East Geopolitical Risk
risks.description: Could boost defense AI demand but also drags high-valuation stocks in broad selloffs.

buyReasons.title: Moder Mortgage + Unstoppable US Expansion Despite European Controversy
buyReasons.rationale: Freedom Mortgage pilot proves financial services entry. US commercial +115% growth continues regardless of European headlines.

buyReasons.title: Triple Bull Case: UBS $200, Wedbush $230, Mizuho $195
buyReasons.rationale: Price drop widened upside to 30%. Mizuho: 'Unlike anything else in software.'

buyReasons.title: Middle East Escalation Could Boost Defense AI Demand
buyReasons.rationale: Pentagon deployment may accelerate defense AI investment. ShipOS and Maven are military AI plays.

buyReasons.title: $11.2B Backlog + Rule of 40 Score 127%
buyReasons.rationale: Maximum revenue visibility. 60% growth + 31% operating margin is extraordinarily rare.

overallOpinion: Palantir launched an AI mortgage platform with Moder and has three analysts simultaneously bullish (UBS $200, Wedbush $230, Mizuho $195), but Middle East escalation and oil surge dragged it down 3% to $151.00.

The Guardian reported Palantir's Swiss magazine lawsuit as an 'intimidation campaign,' and an NHS hospital group chair resigned over a Palantir advisory role. European public contract risk is emerging, though US defense and commercial revenue remain solid.

Below the 50-day ($158) but above the 200-day ($127). Middle East escalation could paradoxically boost defense AI demand. P/E 222x is a burden, but with accelerating growth at Rule of 40 127%, pullbacks are buying opportunities. Buy.

investmentVerdict.summary: Swiss lawsuit and NHS controversy raise European risk, but US defense and commercial expansion remain rock-solid. Moder mortgage partnership, triple analyst upgrades, and 60% revenue growth acceleration are the pillars. P/E 222x is steep, but Rule of 40 at 127% is extraordinarily rare. Middle East escalation could actually boost defense AI demand. Buy on pullbacks.

threads.hook: Palantir: European controversy meets unstoppable US expansion
threads.text:
$PLTR $151.00(-3.01%)

The Guardian reported Palantir's Swiss magazine lawsuit as an 'intimidation campaign,' and an NHS hospital chair resigned over advisory ties. European public contract risk is building.

But in the US, Moder AI mortgage partnership proves financial services entry. UBS $200, Wedbush $230, Mizuho $195 all bullish. Pentagon's Middle East deployment could actually accelerate defense AI spending.

Below 50-day but above 200-day. P/E 222x is steep but Rule of 40 at 127% justifies growth premium. Buy on pullbacks. Target $196(+30%).

empathy.hook: PLTR holders, yesterday's rally erased and then some
empathy.text:
$PLTR $151.00(-3.01%)

Yesterday Palantir was the lone green stock. Today it gave back 3%. Swiss lawsuit headlines, NHS drama, broad market selloff. The whiplash is exhausting.

But the US story hasn't changed. Moder mortgage AI, Mizuho's strongest praise, UBS $200. European noise is real but US defense and commercial are rock-solid.

10 drawdowns of 20%+ in 3 years, all recovered. This too shall pass. DCA on dips. Buy. Target $196(+30%).
